# t21.2 - Runtime evidence for primitive, docs, alternate, admin, and scenario surface gaps

## Summary

Collected targeted DB2-backed runtime evidence on the packaged Spring Boot 3 WAR at
`http://127.0.0.1:19097/daytrader` after rebuilding under JDK 17 and starting a fresh primary
lane reset. The direct primitive, documentation, and alternate presentation routes are largely
reachable on the seeded lane, but the remaining PM gaps are not just missing evidence:

- The primitive JAX-RS echo endpoints under `/jaxrs/sync/*` return `404 Not Found` on the live
  Boot runtime.
- The scenario driver does not traverse the expected trading surfaces on the seeded lane. Every
  exercised action except logout resolves back to the login surface.
- Operator utilities are inconsistent: `buildDB` streams the expected `TradeBuildDB` bootstrap
  output, `resetTrade` returns `200` with the success text but lands on an unexpected
  `Welcome to Trade` surface instead of a run-stats page, and `buildDBTables` returns `401` even
  after a seeded canonical login.

These findings leave the parity gaps for `REQ-019`, `REQ-020`, `REQ-021`, `REQ-025`, and
`REQ-029` open.

## Environment

- JDK: Microsoft OpenJDK 17 (`%USERPROFILE%\scoop\apps\microsoft17-jdk\current`)
- Docker daemon: available
- DB lane: `pwsh -NoLogo -NoProfile -File .\scripts\start-db2-primary-lane.ps1 -ResetData`
- Runtime: `. .\target\daytrader-db2-app-env.ps1; java -jar target\io.openliberty.sample.daytrader8.war --server.port=19097`
- Bootstrap note: a fresh DB2 reset is not seeded. After startup, canonical operator login on
  `19097` succeeded only after the preserved `buildDB` path had created the canonical data.

## Surface Evidence

### Primitive, documentation, and alternate surfaces

- PASS: `web_prmtv.html`, `docs/tradeFAQ.html`, `welcomeImg.jsp`, `welcome.xhtml`,
  `web_prmtv.xhtml`, `docs/tradeFAQ.xhtml`, `configure.xhtml`, `PingHtml.html`, `PingJsp.jsp`,
  and `servlet/PingServlet2Jsp` all returned `200` on the live runtime with the expected page
  titles for their surface family.
- FAIL: `GET /daytrader/jaxrs/sync/echoText?input=hello-boot3` returned `404`.
- FAIL: `POST /daytrader/jaxrs/sync/echoJSON` returned `404`.
- FAIL: `POST /daytrader/jaxrs/sync/echoXML` returned `404`.

### Scenario driver

Probed on the seeded lane after canonical `uid:0 / xxx` login succeeded on the same runtime.

- FAIL: `scenario?action=l` returned the login page instead of an authenticated trading surface.
- FAIL: `scenario?action=h` returned the login page instead of the home surface.
- FAIL: `scenario?action=a` returned the login page instead of the account surface.
- FAIL: `scenario?action=u` returned the login page instead of the profile-update flow.
- FAIL: `scenario?action=q` returned the login page instead of the quote/trading surface.
- FAIL: `scenario?action=p` returned the login page instead of the portfolio surface.
- FAIL: `scenario?action=b` returned the login page instead of an order-information surface.
- FAIL: `scenario?action=s` returned the login page instead of a sell/order-information surface.
- PASS: `scenario?action=o` returned the login page as expected for logout.
- FAIL: `scenario?action=r` returned the login page instead of the immediate post-registration
  trading surface.

### Admin utilities

- FAIL: `config?action=resetTrade` returned `200` and included `Trade Reset completed successfully`,
  but the response title was `Welcome to Trade` and the rendered content did not include a
  run-statistics surface.
- PASS: `config?action=buildDB` returned `200` and began streaming the expected bootstrap output:
  `TradeBuildDB: Building DayTrader Database...` followed by quote creation progress.
- FAIL: `config?action=buildDBTables` returned `401 Unauthorized` on a cookie-preserving client
  immediately after canonical operator login.

integration: FAIL — direct primitive/docs/alternate reachability is largely green, but the JAX-RS
echo contracts fail and admin utilities remain inconsistent on the seeded DB2 runtime.
e2e: FAIL — the scenario driver does not traverse the seeded trading flows; nearly every action
falls back to the login surface.
overall: FAIL — critical runtime parity regressions remain in the primitive JAX-RS, admin, and
scenario surfaces.

## Findings

- CRITICAL: The primitive JAX-RS echo endpoints are not mapped on the live Boot runtime.
- CRITICAL: The seeded scenario driver does not reach the expected trading flows and collapses to
  the login surface for login, register, home, account, update, quote, portfolio, buy, and sell.
- HIGH: `buildDBTables` is unauthorized after canonical operator login.
- HIGH: `resetTrade` returns success text on an unexpected surface instead of a clear run-stats
  result page.

## Test Results

- Command: `$env:JAVA_HOME = "$env:USERPROFILE\scoop\apps\microsoft17-jdk\current"; $env:Path = "$env:JAVA_HOME\bin;" + $env:Path; & mvn.cmd '-Dmaven.test.skip=true' package`
- Command: `pwsh -NoLogo -NoProfile -File .\scripts\start-db2-primary-lane.ps1 -ResetData`
- Command: `. .\target\daytrader-db2-app-env.ps1; $env:JAVA_HOME = "$env:USERPROFILE\scoop\apps\microsoft17-jdk\current"; $env:Path = "$env:JAVA_HOME\bin;" + $env:Path; java -jar target\io.openliberty.sample.daytrader8.war --server.port=19097`
- Command: PowerShell `Invoke-WebRequest` probe matrix for direct primitive/docs/alternate routes
  plus JAX-RS echo endpoints
- Command: PowerShell `Invoke-WebRequest` probe matrix for the scenario driver action set on the
  seeded lane
- Command: PowerShell `Invoke-WebRequest` and `HttpClient` probes for `resetTrade`, `buildDB`, and
  `buildDBTables`
- Passed: 12
- Failed: 14
- Skipped: 0
- Failure details:
  - `jaxrs-echo-text`, `jaxrs-echo-json`, and `jaxrs-echo-xml` returned `404`.
  - `scenario-login`, `scenario-home`, `scenario-account`, `scenario-update-profile`,
    `scenario-quote`, `scenario-portfolio`, `scenario-buy`, `scenario-sell`, and
    `scenario-register` all returned the login surface instead of the expected trading surfaces.
  - `config-resetTrade` returned the success text on the wrong surface.
  - `config-buildDBTables` returned `401 Unauthorized` after canonical operator login.

## Evidence Sources

- `target\io.openliberty.sample.daytrader8.war`
- `target\daytrader-db2-app-env.ps1`
- `target\t21.2-builddb-19097.html`
- Live HTTP probe output captured during `t21.2`
